title: Tarenna malacophylla
taxonomy: Tarenna malacophylla is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The family Rubiaceae includes around 13,000 to 15,000 species of plants. Tarenna malacophylla is native to the island of Madagascar. It is a part of the genus Tarenna, which includes around 400 species of flowering plants.

intro: Tarenna malacophylla, also known as Malabar Tarenna, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. Research has shown that Tarenna malacophylla possesses various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help in treating conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
